title: astragalus clevelandii
intro: Astragalus clevelandii, also known as Cleveland's milkvetch, is a perennial legume native to the southwestern United States. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting effects, making it a popular ingredient in traditional herbal remedies. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing stress, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. Astragalus clevelandii can be consumed as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its effects.
